name: Giblet Gravy
recipeInstructions:
1. Place giblets and neck from turkey in a saucepan and cover with water.
2. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 to 30 minutes.
3. Drain the giblet stock and reserve it. Cut the giblets into small cubes and reserve them.
4. When the turkey is done and removed from the roasting pan, add at least 1 cup of water to the pan. Blend the water with the drippings, stirring and scraping with a wooden spoon to loosen any browned bits that may have stuck to the bottom of the pan. The water and pan drippings together should equal 2 cups. Reserve this liquid.
5. In a saucepan, melt butter. Whisk in flour and cook over medium heat until golden brown. Stir in the reserved giblet stock and liquid from the pan drippings. Stir constantly until thickened, to avoid lumping.
6. Season with salt and pepper, stir in the giblet cubes, and serve with roast turkey.
7. Makes 2 cups.
Posted by Stephen Ceideberg; December 13, 1991.
